name: project
description: Per-project atlas + map + Kanban board with hierarchical tasks, workstream-tagged cards, and All-Projects hub. v1.4.0 MINOR (v0.31.0 S6.5): hub note filename now defaults to the user-typed project name (e.g. 'Testing It All Out.md' under spice/projects/testing-it-all-out/) for native Obsidian tab title display. Configurable HUB_NOTE_FILENAME_STYLE constant in project-nav-buttons.js (name|slug|fixed). Rule_fragment refactored to type-discrimination via frontmatter_branch so audit + hub-cards filters are filename-independent. v1.4.1 PATCH CF-1: projects-hub-cards.js filter restored to match EITHER type:project OR legacy #project tag (older hub notes without type:project frontmatter were excluded from the Projects.md card grid); defensive type:map/kanban exclusions handle dataview's nested-tag expansion (#project/<slug> would otherwise falsely include Project Map + Project Board). v1.5.0 MINOR (v0.33.0 cohesion wave 2): adopts claude_surface[] — ships canonical /project slash command + new-project SKILL.md + resolvers row. Stale consumer-side .claude/commands/project.md bodies overwritten on next install. v1.6.0 MINOR (v0.39.0): NEW frontmatter fields status (7-state enum, required) + status_changed_at (YYYY-MM-DD, required) + teams[] + products[] (both optional wikilink lists). Default-active hub scope hides terminal statuses (superseded/cancelled/done). Tier 1 action buttons (Bump Status / Set Teams / Set Products) ship via ProjectActionButtons customjs class (S7).

post_install.message: Project blueprint v1.0.0 installed at spice/projects/. To create your first project: open spice/projects/Projects.md (Cmd+O → search 'Projects') and click the 'New Project' action button. Per-project Kanban cards auto-promote to folder-style (tasks/<X>/<X>.md and board/<Y>/<Y>.md) via Templater. Workstream tagging on cards via the Change/Assign widget rendered on each card. Cmd+R required after this install to load the 3 CustomJS classes.

bootstrap_contributions.prompt: Filter expression for this engagement's primary kanban (blank = no filter)
